news_subtitle: Materials about 3D modeling, AI content generation and their interaction.
about_subtitle: For over 12 years I have been engaged in artistic 3D modeling, with 5 years of art school behind me, plus additional individual training in sculpture and low relief from professionals in the field. Now my main focus is low relief (coins, medals, signs and badges), bas-reliefs. My studio represents a fusion of traditional craftsmanship with the integration of artificial intelligence into individual stages of the workflow.
about_p2: For a long time I have been creating detailed 3D models of medals, coins and bas-reliefs, artistic patterns and sculptures, souvenir items, while simultaneously exploring the boundaries of AI-generated images and elements. My work combines classical art and modern technologies. Each 3D model begins with studying the historical context and continues with meticulous digital sculpting. In parallel, AI tools help visualize ideas and find directions that are difficult to achieve using only traditional methods.
about_p3: Need a custom commemorative medal, badge, souvenir coin, just a consultation on 3D modeling services, a creative partner for a project — I will help bring your idea to life. I maintain contact both via the specified email and through the feedback form.
about_p4: Among my clients and partners are organisations such as Spetsznak, the Moscow Dynamo Museum, the city administration, government bodies, medals for Russian Orthodox Church institutions, work with dental organisations, and many private and corporate clients. Work is performed under a contract between sole proprietor Kurilenko A.A. and the client; assignment and use of copyright follow applicable Russian law. A short overview of copyright and transfer terms is available in the relevant section of the site.

forum_topic_title: Best settings for bas relief in Blender 4.3?
forum_topic_author_meta: by Mikhail Sorokin · 3D Modeling · 2 hours ago
forum_topic_body: I've been trying to print some of the medal models at home with my FDM printer and I'm losing a lot of the fine detail. Anyone have experience with resin vs FDM for this kind of work? What settings give the best results?
forum_reply_1: Great question! For FDM, I'd recommend: 0.12mm layer height, no supports needed for flat surfaces, PLA+ or PETG, and slow print speed (30mm/s).
forum_reply_2: Resin printer will give you much better results for fine detail. The Elegoo Saturn is great for this price range.
